back_translation: For the choirmaster. To the tune of 'Do Not Destroy.' A Miktam of David.
Do you indeed speak justly, O rulers? Do you judge the sons of men with equity?
No, in your hearts you devise injustice; your hands deal out violence throughout the land.
The wicked go astray from the womb; they err from birth, speaking lies.
Their venom is like the venom of a serpent, like a deaf cobra that stops up its ear,
that will not hear the voice of charmers, however skillful the enchanter may be.
O God, break the teeth in their mouths; tear out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.
Let them vanish like water that flows away; when he bends his bow, let his arrows be as if blunted.
Let them be like a snail that melts away as it goes, like a stillborn child that never sees the sun.
Before your pots can feel the burning thorns—whether green or ablaze—He will sweep them away.
The righteous will rejoice when he sees vengeance; he will bathe his feet in the blood of the wicked.
Then mankind will say, 'Surely there is a reward for the righteous; surely there is a God who judges the earth.'
